---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Hila Shmuel <>
Date: Thu, May 13, 2010 at 3:28 PM
Subject: Hila Q1 13.5.2010
To:
explanation: The code defines a graph using adjacency lists and processes the nodes to determine the minimal number of colored children for each node, using a recursive depth-first search approach. It calculates the number of child nodes that can be colored black or white, ensuring the node coloring adheres to specific constraints where a white node requires all of its children to be black and a black node can selectively allow some children to be white. Finally, it prints the result based on the optimal coloring of the root node.
name: 2010-05-13_hila-q1-13-5-2010
subject: Fwd: Hila Q1 13.5.2010
summary: This project implements a solution to a graph coloring problem, where nodes can be colored black or white to minimize the weight of the tree structure.
